edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback template is structurally distinct from ema_rsi_atr: it requires price above EMA_200 (structural uptrend) + EMA_50 slope rising (intermediate momentum) + RSI pulled back below threshold (oversold dip). This 'buy the dip in a confirmed uptrend' logic targets mean-reversion entries with trend continuation. Prior RSI pullback test (PF=1.16, 51 trades) showed trending PF=1.51 and ranging PF=1.40 — good signals but destroyed by DD=1.84 from tight exits. Applying the validated wide-exit fee-killer philosophy (3.0-4.0x ATR TP) should let pullback recoveries run to full continuation. Deep pullback (RSI<35) selects stronger mean-reversion setups with higher recovery velocity. Regime assumption: TRENDING (price > EMA_200 is inherently a trending filter). Invalidation: if PF < 1.0 with wide exits, the pullback signal has no edge — RSI dips in uptrends don't reliably recover on ETH.

variants.name: rsi_deep_pullback_wide_exit
variants.description: Deep RSI pullback (RSI<35) in structural uptrend (close > EMA_200, EMA_50 slope up) with wide exits. Deeper pullback threshold selects stronger oversold conditions with higher snap-back probability. Wide TP (3.0x ATR) lets the recovery run. Prior test used RSI<40 default — tightening to 35 filters out marginal pullbacks.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (EMA_50 current > EMA_50 previous bar).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (EMA_50 current < EMA_50 previous bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit long when RSI_14 > 70 (overbought — pullback recovery complete).
Exit short when RSI_14 < 30 (oversold — downtrend pullback recovery complete).
Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit if close drops below EMA_200 (structural trend invalidated).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Deep pullbacks may signal trend exhaustion, not mean-reversion
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 structural trend + EMA_50 slope for momentum confirmation
RSI_14 <= 35 deep pullback entry in uptrend
ATR_14 for wide exit sizing (3.0x TP / 1.5x stop)

variants.name: rsi_pullback_ultra_wide_fee_killer
variants.description: RSI pullback with maximum fee-killer exit structure (4.0x ATR TP / 2.0x ATR stop, 2:1 R:R). Uses default RSI<40 for more trade volume. Hypothesis: the prior RSI pullback DD=1.84 came from tight exits causing repeated small losses; wide exits let winners run large enough to overcome fee drag and absorb losing streaks. Mirrors the exit philosophy that pushed MACD from PF=1.03 to PF=1.35.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (EMA_50 current > EMA_50 previous bar).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (EMA_50 current < EMA_50 previous bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit long when RSI_14 > 75 (strong overbought — take profit).
Exit short when RSI_14 < 25 (strong oversold — take profit).
Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (4.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit if close drops below EMA_200 (structural trend invalidated).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Ultra-wide exits may reduce trade frequency below minimum
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 structural trend + EMA_50 slope confirmation
RSI_14 <= 40 standard pullback — fee killer exits
ATR_14 for ultra-wide exits (4.0x TP / 2.0x stop)
